nodes.label: Hexagonal / Clean Architecture Dependency Rule
nodes.rationale: Dependency rule domain -> app -> infra & api: domain never imports outward, app uses ports while infra provides adapters — keeps the core pure, framework-free, and unit-testable with zero I/O.

nodes.label: Domain Freeze (Phase 0)
nodes.rationale: The domain is frozen so contracts change only via deliberate, decision-logged un-freezes; recorded un-freezes: #1 AgentSpec runtime fields (2026-07-05), #2 planning backoff gate (2026-07-08), #3 pause gate + navigation strictness (2026-07-09).

nodes.label: Nine-Phase Plan Lifecycle (PlanPhase)
nodes.rationale: DISCOVERY→ARCHITECTURE→ENRICHING→AWAITING_REVIEW→RUNNING→REVIEW→DONE with REPLANNING re-entering ARCHITECTURE and FAILED terminal; each phase belongs to exactly one driver (chat, worker, or human gate) so nothing spins waiting for input.

nodes.label: Pause Gate (un-freeze #3)
nodes.rationale: Plan.paused is a claim-predicate flag, not a phase: human pause or auto-pause on task exhaustion stops the worker from claiming and makes goals/tasks editable; resume clears it and requeues FAILED tasks with a fresh budget. Replaced the deleted fail_goal so terminal FAILED is only reachable via fail_plan.

nodes.label: Transactional Outbox Pattern
nodes.rationale: State changes and their DomainEvents are written in the same transaction so state and events can never diverge; side effects (agent runs, LLM calls) happen outside transactions and finalize transactions re-read and re-guard.

nodes.label: Shared Failure Taxonomy (FailureKind)
nodes.rationale: connection_error/rate_limit/timeout/tool_error are retryable; token_limit/auth_error are terminal. Produced by both the CLI runners and the dry-run dummy so dry-run exercises the production retry paths.

nodes.label: Git Worktree Workspace Rules
nodes.rationale: Each attempt runs in a worktree on task/<task_id>/a<attempt> off plan/<plan_id>; commit is a --no-ff merge, discard deletes worktree + branch so a failed attempt leaves zero trace (stateless task execution). main is never touched.

nodes.label: Two Event Streams, One Delivery Path
nodes.rationale: Coarse outbox events are transactional with state; fine agent_events telemetry is best-effort on its own connection — both are delivered through the relay to the one SSE stream.
nodes.norm_label: two event streams, one delivery path

nodes.label: Dry-Run + Stub Default Mode
nodes.rationale: The default mode (dry-run runner + stub reasoner) exercises the entire system without any API key; neither dry-run path ever touches the secret store, so no master key is needed until going real. The reasoner and runner switches are orthogonal.

nodes.label: H1: Double-Execution Window Defect
nodes.rationale: The default worker lease (300s) is shorter than the default task timeout (600s) and heartbeats only happen between units, so a long-running task can be claimed and executed a second time concurrently; fix is a heartbeat task around drive_plan plus a boot-time lease clamp.

nodes.label: H2: Poisoned-Plan Starvation Defect
nodes.rationale: A plan whose drive raises before any save keeps updated_at unchanged, so the ORDER BY updated_at claim query picks it first every tick forever, starving other plans; fix is release() setting updated_at = now to rotate failing plans to the back of the queue.

nodes.label: Goal-Level Parallelism (lease granularity)
nodes.rationale: The lease granularity (plan -> goal -> task) is the designed parallelism switch; do not bolt a queue on top — move the lease. Requires next_action returning a set of ready units and a merge-conflict strategy; the Goal.depends_on DAG seam already exists unused.

nodes.label: Attempt History on the Task
nodes.rationale: requeue() and Task.retry() erase the failed attempt's result; a bounded attempt_history would preserve it but requires a deliberate one-field domain un-freeze. More visible now that resume-as-retry resets attempt to 0.

nodes.label: Planned CI Pipeline (per-PR vs nightly split)
nodes.rationale: Per-PR: unit + integration + dummy e2e + ruff/mypy; nightly/merge-only: the paid real-model smoke — the split exists so money isn't burned per push.
nodes.norm_label: planned ci pipeline (per-pr vs nightly split)

nodes.label: Do-Not-Do List (rejected improvements)
nodes.rationale: Explicitly rejected: Temporal/DBOS/Celery/Redis (adds a server dependency to a local-first tool and makes the gated phase machine harder to test); task-level parallelism (breaks the plan-document CAS for speculative gain); splitting the plan JSON into relational rows (the single document + dual-backend truth tests are the core asset); WebSockets/SSE replay (refetch-on-connect + event_id dedup suffice).

nodes.label: Reasoner Port (converse / enrich_goal)
nodes.rationale: Exactly two methods: converse is multi-turn with commit (goals=None keeps the conversation open, a goal list is the roadmap commit); enrich_goal breaks one goal into tasks. User messages persist before the LLM call so they survive reasoner crashes.

nodes.label: StubReasoner (deterministic grammar)
nodes.rationale: Deterministic 'ask:' / 'goal:/task: [caps: ...]' grammar drives dry-run and tests without any API key.

nodes.label: OpenAIReasoner (tool-calling agent loop)
nodes.rationale: run_tool_session with terminal submit tools (submit_goals, submit_tasks), {accepted:false, errors} self-correction, transient/permanent retry classification, empty-choices guard, plan-to-markdown context renderer. Handlers re-validate every tool argument; history replays as plain text, never provider transcripts.

nodes.label: Version CAS (optimistic concurrency)
nodes.rationale: Use cases bump_version() before save(); the store rejects stored.version >= incoming.version via a conditional upsert (rowcount 0 -> StaleVersionError), catching the worker-vs-edit race. save() never touches lease columns.

nodes.label: ARCHITECTURE No-LLM Passthrough
nodes.rationale: The discovery/replanning conversation already committed the user-agreed roadmap, so an autonomous re-structuring pass would be redundant and risks mangling a goal set the user just signed off. The phase stays in the frozen enum as a free crash checkpoint and REPLANNING re-entry point; the handler is the seam if a real structuring pass returns.

nodes.label: ENRICHING Just-in-Time Goal Breakdown
nodes.rationale: One task-less goal per handle(); a goal that already has tasks is never re-enriched — the idempotency guard for crashes and lease races. Goals the user populated in chat are skipped; no task-less goal left means bind_agents -> AWAITING_REVIEW.

nodes.label: AgentRunner Port
nodes.rationale: async run(task, spec, idempotency_key, event_sink, workspace) -> TaskResult; raises TaskFailed with the shared FailureKind taxonomy. The classifier is conservative: unknown output becomes TOOL_ERROR (retryable). The dummy runner emits the same kinds so dry-run exercises production paths.

nodes.label: Workspace Port (git branching = the rollback)
nodes.rationale: begin() creates branch + worktree, commit() does the --no-ff merge into the plan branch, discard() removes worktree and branch — zero trace, retries begin clean (stateless task execution).

nodes.label: The Truth Test (dual-backend verification)
nodes.rationale: The in-memory orchestration suite runs against the real SQLite UnitOfWork via the parametrized env_factory fixture; crash-recovery-via-lease-expiry, outbox-rollback, and backoff-survives-crash passing on real SQLite prove transactional atomicity is real, not simulated.

nodes.label: Chat Persistence (plan_chat_messages)
nodes.rationale: Append-only per-plan history written on its own short transactions, never inside the plan UoW: a lost display reply never loses plan state and a state rollback never erases what the user said.

nodes.label: SSEBroker (per-client queue fan-out)
nodes.rationale: Per-client asyncio.Queue fan-out with thread-safe publish (off-loop callers hop via call_soon_threadsafe); routers never call it — only the relay publishes.

nodes.label: Outbox Relay Thread
nodes.rationale: Undelivered outbox rows -> broker -> mark delivered; publish-then-mark gives at-least-once delivery. The same thread tails agent_events by cursor into the 'agent.event' SSE stream. Without it, events are written but never seen.

nodes.label: Single Error-to-HTTP Mapping (_STATUS_BY_CODE)
nodes.rationale: Every DomainError carries a stable code; adding an error type is one table line, never a new handler. Deliberately no blanket KeyError/ValueError mapping — an unmapped builtin is a bug and surfaces as an enveloped 500.

nodes.label: PlanDispatcher (advance_plan phase router)
nodes.rationale: Thin phase-to-handler router returning a Signal per unit of work; replaced the old god-function so task execution, planning, and gates can't disturb each other.

nodes.label: GateHandler (unconditional PAUSED)
nodes.rationale: Returns PAUSED unconditionally — the old conditional check was the verified gate-spin bug.

nodes.label: Worker Loop (worker_tick / drive_plan)
nodes.rationale: claim (lease) -> drive_plan (while CONTINUE: advance; heartbeat) -> release in finally. The tick returns progress, not claiming — a zero-step claim returns False so the caller sleeps (the verified spin fix). Crash recovery is lease expiry + any worker's next claim.

nodes.label: Conversation Use Cases (discovery/replanning turns)
nodes.rationale: Per turn: guard phase on fresh read -> persist the user message before the LLM call (own short chat txn) -> reasoner.converse outside any txn -> re-guard before committing goals + phase atomically. Chat is display history; the plan transaction is truth — neither can roll the other back.

nodes.label: Crash-Safety Choreography (ExecutionHandler rules)
nodes.rationale: Check-before-act idempotency (a task with a result is finalized without re-running the agent); two-transaction write with the agent side effect outside any txn; no live aggregate refs across txn boundaries; tolerant finalize for late results after a replan; durable backoff gate via retry_not_before; retry-vs-terminal decided by RetryPolicy; agent resolved before RUNNING.

nodes.label: In-Memory Fakes (testing/fakes.py)
nodes.rationale: InMemoryPlanRepository (CAS + lease semantics identical to SQLite), DummyAgentRunner (scripted per task id, shared failure taxonomy), InMemoryChatStore, FakeClock — the whole loop runs with zero infrastructure; infra re-exports the dummy as the dry-run runtime.

nodes.label: Navigation Derived, Never Stored
nodes.rationale: There is no cursor: 'what runs next' is computed every time by scanning node statuses, so a crash (reload + re-scan) or an edit (insert/reorder) can never desync. This is what makes the old reconciler unnecessary — unready work is simply never selected.

nodes.label: Plan Aggregate Root
nodes.rationale: Single consistency boundary owning the goal/task tree; only Plan calls entity transitions because cross-entity rules (start_task idempotency, cascade start of a PENDING goal, terminal guard, goal-failure halts plan) must be enforced in one place — the classic DDD rule that outside callers touch only the root.

nodes.label: Append-Only Replan Loop
nodes.rationale: begin_replanning() skips the iteration's PENDING work; commit_replanned_goals() finalize-abandons non-terminal leftovers, appends new goals, bumps iteration, and flows into ARCHITECTURE. Prior DONE goals are never touched — they are history and re-plan context.

nodes.label: Cooperative Pausing (between units, never mid-run)
nodes.rationale: Pausing is checked by the loop between task units, never mid-run: the domain never kills a live agent. The worker finishes the current task, persists, then pauses by releasing the lease; force-killing a running agent is an adapter/process concern deliberately not modeled in the domain.

nodes.label: Task Entity (guarded self-transitions + backoff gate)
nodes.rationale: start() accepts PENDING or RUNNING (idempotent re-pick after a crash); requeue() clears result, preserves attempt, and sets the durable retry_not_before gate. required_capabilities are catalog ids, not embedded entities, so catalog data never goes stale inside tasks. is_ready_at(now) is a method because now is injected — the domain never reads the clock.

nodes.label: Goal Entity (status derived from tasks)
nodes.rationale: Only start/complete/fail — no retry or stop on purpose: retry/backoff is a task concern, and pausing is a worker-loop concern. depends_on is the unused DAG seam for future parallelism.

nodes.label: AgentSpec Entity (role + model_role tiers)
nodes.rationale: role is the functional job; model_role is an indirection key naming a model tier resolved against the provider catalog at runtime, so swapping the model behind a tier doesn't touch every agent that uses it.

nodes.label: DomainError base (stable codes + log-safe context)
nodes.rationale: One exception per failure case, each carrying a stable code for programmatic handling / API mapping and a log-safe context dict; never put secrets in context.

nodes.label: Delete-Guard Integrity Rule (cascade down, guard up)
nodes.rationale: Refuse to delete an agent/model/provider/capability still referenced by a non-terminal task or active plan (ReferencedEntityInUseError); a provider delete cascades to its models but is guarded if a model is in use. Even with the guard, execution re-checks and fails cleanly on a dangling agent reference (AgentNotFoundError as the reactive net).
nodes.norm_label: delete-guard integrity rule (cascade down, guard up)

nodes.label: Coarse Outbox Events (transactional)
nodes.rationale: State transitions written to the outbox in the same transaction as the state change so state and event can never diverge. Payloads stay id-only (minimal-payload rule); plan_id is the correlation handle; events carry no default log message because human-readable text is a presentation concern.

nodes.label: Fine Agent Events (best-effort telemetry)
nodes.rationale: Tool calls, steps, tokens streamed mid-run; not transactional with state, tagged by attempt so a re-run after a crash is distinguishable in the live view.

nodes.label: event_id Dedup (at-least-once to effectively-once)
nodes.rationale: An at-least-once relay can deliver the same event twice, so each consumer records processed event_ids and skips repeats — no double side-effect when the relay crashes between publish and mark-sent.

nodes.label: create()/reconstruct() Factory Split
nodes.rationale: create() builds from zero, runs birth invariants and generates ids; reconstruct() rebuilds from persisted state, trusting the stored data and never regenerating ids or re-applying defaults. The repository calls the factory, never the reverse. Thin factories are kept because create() centralizes id generation and the uniform shape is worth more than the lines saved.

nodes.label: identity.new_id() (centralized id generation)
nodes.rationale: Single source of id generation so the strategy (uuid now, something else later) is centralized instead of scattered across call sites.

nodes.label: RetryPolicy (retry/backoff decision)
nodes.rationale: Owns the decision (retry or terminal? how long?); the adapter owns the mechanism (waiting, re-invoking). Persisted per-plan so it's configurable and survives crashes — making retries-exhausted-terminal a domain rule rather than adapter magic.

nodes.label: backoff_for(attempt) exponential backoff
nodes.rationale: attempt is 1-based; retry_index = attempt - 1 and the exponent is retry_index - 1 so the first retry pays the base delay (multiplier**0 = 1x), not an already-scaled one. Capped at max_backoff_seconds.

nodes.label: PlanRepository Port
nodes.rationale: One contract covering persistence (get/save with the version CAS), create idempotency (find_by_request_id/bind_request_id so a retried create returns the same plan id), and the lease primitives.

nodes.label: Plan Lease (claim_one_unit / heartbeat / release)
nodes.rationale: The lease lives on the repository port, not the aggregate, because it is cross-process coordination — it needs atomic storage operations and a clock, exactly the infrastructure concerns the pure aggregate must not know. An expired lease makes a dead worker's plan reclaimable; this replaces the old reconciler.

nodes.label: next_action(goals, now) — the navigation scan
nodes.rationale: A failed task does not immediately fail its goal: the scan drains every actionable task first, and only a goal with no actionable tasks and at least one FAILED task returns the GOAL_FAILED signal — so a transient failure with retries left never fails the goal. now is injected so the scan stays pure and deterministic.

nodes.label: Edit Service (structural edit rules)
nodes.rationale: Edit validation is domain logic spanning plan structure and request; a RUNNING or terminal goal is not editable — which is also why the scan can never desync: only not-yet-started work is editable. _renumber keeps positions dense; remove_task filters by id so a miss raises a typed InvalidEditError.

nodes.label: match_agent (pure capability matcher)
nodes.rationale: First agent whose capability ids cover the task's required ids; returns (agent_id, used_default). A free function, not a Task method, so it's trivially testable.

nodes.label: find_goal / find_task (shared lookups)
nodes.rationale: Pure in-memory traversals of an already-loaded aggregate — there is no goal/task repo because goals and tasks are owned by the Plan root and loaded/saved whole. Extracted so the aggregate and edit_service share one find-or-raise implementation with consistent domain errors.

nodes.label: Status enum + TERMINAL set
nodes.rationale: Lifecycle enum shared by both Goal and Task; TERMINAL = {DONE, SKIPPED, FAILED}. A FAILED node being terminal is what stops the old infinite loop — it is skipped, never re-selected forever.

nodes.label: TaskResult (typed output + idempotency record)
nodes.rationale: If task.result is set, the work already happened and must not re-execute. artifacts is the flexible per-task-type payload so one rigid schema isn't forced on every task type. The seam that makes orchestration deterministically testable: tests fabricate TaskResults by hand, production builds them from the agent, and orchestration treats both identically.

nodes.label: Execution Model (worker, lease, crash choreography, workspace)
nodes.norm_label: execution model (worker, lease, crash choreography, workspace)

nodes.label: ADR-001: Concurrency Model — the per-plan lease
nodes.norm_label: adr-001: concurrency model — the per-plan lease

nodes.label: Analysis 2026-06-10: Spec API + discovery fixes (old backend)
nodes.norm_label: analysis 2026-06-10: spec api + discovery fixes (old backend)

nodes.label: Analysis 2026-06-13: Planner provider + frontend gaps (old backend)
nodes.norm_label: analysis 2026-06-13: planner provider + frontend gaps (old backend)

nodes.label: Nine-Phase Plan Machine
nodes.rationale: DISCOVERY→ARCHITECTURE→ENRICHING→AWAITING_REVIEW→RUNNING→REVIEW→DONE with REPLANNING re-entering ARCHITECTURE and FAILED terminal. ENRICHING is separate from ARCHITECTURE for crash-recovery granularity: more phase boundaries = more checkpoints = finer resume. DONE is reachable only via REVIEW 'finish'.

nodes.label: Driver Model (who advances each phase)
nodes.rationale: Each phase has exactly one driver (chat / worker / human gate command); the worker claim predicate makes non-worker phases invisible to workers, so a plan waiting on chat or a human can never cause claim/release churn.

nodes.label: ARCHITECTURE No-LLM Passthrough
nodes.rationale: A decision, not a gap: the conversation already commits the user-agreed goal roadmap, so an autonomous re-structuring pass would be redundant and risks mangling a goal set the user just signed off. The phase stays in the frozen enum because REPLANNING re-enters through it (a free crash checkpoint) and the handler is the seam if a real structuring pass returns.

nodes.label: JIT Enrichment (one task-less goal per step)
nodes.rationale: One task-less goal per worker step, checkpointed via Signal.CONTINUE. The idempotency guard — a goal that already has tasks is never re-enriched — absorbs both crashes between the LLM call and the commit, and racing workers.
nodes.norm_label: jit enrichment (one task-less goal per step)

nodes.label: Conversational Phases (multi-turn with commit)
nodes.rationale: A reply without goals keeps the conversation open; a reply with goals IS the roadmap commit. User message persists before the LLM call so it survives reasoner crashes. Chat is display history on its own transactions; the plan transaction is truth — a lost reply never loses plan state. Chat replies travel in the HTTP body; SSE carries only domain events.

nodes.label: Append-Only Replan Loop
nodes.rationale: PENDING work is skipped at request time; in-flight results land tolerantly (late failure terminal-skips — never requeues into an abandoned iteration, the resurrection bug); commit-time finalize-abandon closes leftovers, goals append after history, iteration increments in exactly one place. Prior DONE goals are simultaneously audit history and the reasoner's re-plan context.

nodes.label: Per-Plan Lease (sequential per plan)
nodes.rationale: Crash recovery stays trivial: one lease, one owner, expired lease = reclaimable — the lease is the entire replacement for the old reconciler. The version-CAS on the plan is the single write gate, and the workspace has no merge-conflict strategy yet, so sequential-per-plan now. The lease GRANULARITY (plan → goal → task) is the deliberate future parallelism switch: move the lease, don't bolt a queue on top.

nodes.label: Worker Claim/Drive/Release Loop
nodes.rationale: The tick reports progress, not claiming — a claim yielding zero steps sleeps (returning True there produced a verified hot claim→release CPU spin). A tick exception never kills the worker. Crash recovery is the lease, nothing else — no supervisor, no reconciler.

nodes.label: Pull-Scan Navigation (next_action)
nodes.rationale: Pure function, no stored cursor — every step re-derives the frontier by scanning statuses, so there is no cursor to desync. Readiness conditions make nodes skipped, not stuck; backoff is a persisted retry_not_before timestamp the scan honors, never a sleep. Strict in-goal order: a backing-off head task blocks its whole goal. now is injected — the domain never reads a clock.

nodes.label: Two-Transaction Choreography
nodes.rationale: State transitions are transactional; side effects (agent runs) are not; finalize re-reads and re-guards. Check-before-act idempotency covers a crash after the agent returned but before finalize. No live aggregate reference crosses a transaction boundary (the frozen _Unit snapshot). Tolerant finalize prevents requeueing into an abandoned iteration.

nodes.label: Pause Gate (paused flag, auto-pause, resume = manual retry)
nodes.rationale: Plan.paused is a durable claim-predicate flag, not a phase (un-freeze #3). Auto-pause replaced terminal fail_goal so a terminal task failure is a recoverable needs-attention halt: goal stays open, plan stays RUNNING+paused, goals/tasks become editable, and resume() is the manual retry (FAILED tasks back to PENDING with fresh attempt budget, bypassing should_retry). Pause latches at the unit boundary and finalize is CAS-protected, so there is no torn write.
nodes.norm_label: pause gate (paused flag, auto-pause, resume = manual retry)

nodes.label: Shared Failure Taxonomy (FailureKind)
nodes.rationale: connection_error/rate_limit/timeout/tool_error retryable; token_limit/auth_error terminal (identical on every retry / config never fixes itself). Classification is conservative — anything unrecognized is retryable TOOL_ERROR, because misclassifying transient-as-terminal kills a plan while the reverse merely wastes a retry. Produced by BOTH real CLI runners and the dry-run dummy so dry-run exercises the exact production retry paths.

nodes.label: Git-Branching Workspace as Rollback
nodes.rationale: Worktree per attempt on task/<id>/a<n> off plan/<plan_id>; commit = --no-ff merge (preserves the attempt in history); discard = worktree + branch deleted — a failed attempt leaves zero trace and the retry begins clean (stateless task execution). main is never touched by plan work; GitHub PR output deferred behind the Workspace port.

nodes.label: Plan-as-Document (one JSON document in plans.data)
nodes.rationale: The whole aggregate is one JSON document; scalar columns are promoted only for what SQL must predicate on. Buys detached aggregates for free (every get() parses fresh JSON — a returned Plan can never alias stored state, making fake and real repositories behave identically) and one write gate (plan-level version CAS). Cost: write amplification — every task transition rewrites the whole document.

nodes.label: Version CAS (optimistic concurrency)
nodes.rationale: bump_version() then save(); the UPDATE arm fires only when stored version < incoming; rowcount 0 → StaleVersionError → HTTP 409 — the worker-vs-human-edit race is surfaced, never silently lost. save() never touches the lease columns: ownership and state travel on separate paths.

nodes.label: Re-enterable SqliteUnitOfWork
nodes.rationale: Each `with uow:` opens a fresh Session + transaction. State and outbox rows commit atomically because the open transaction IS the outbox staging area — a rollback discards both. Chat and telemetry deliberately do NOT use the plan UoW so display history and plan truth can never roll each other back.

nodes.label: Envelope-Encrypted Secrets
nodes.rationale: ciphertext + per-secret data key wrapped by the master Fernet key; catalog rows carry only api_key_ref URIs. resolve() is the single decryption point and fails closed on a missing master key — which is why stub/dry-run factories receive the store as an unevaluated thunk and never construct it.

nodes.label: Transactional Outbox
nodes.rationale: Domain events are written inside the state transaction — an event exists iff its state change committed. Routers never publish; the row IS the notification, durably, which makes 'state changed but nobody was told' impossible. Payloads are minimal (IDs + tiny metadata); consumers refetch state.

nodes.label: agent_events Telemetry Stream
nodes.rationale: Fine-grained per-attempt runtime facts plus plan-scoped llm.call token-usage rows (nullable task_id). Best-effort, own connection, INSERT OR IGNORE dedup — deliberately split from the outbox because losing telemetry must never roll back plan state, and plan state committing must never block on a telemetry write.

nodes.label: Outbox Relay → SSE Delivery
nodes.rationale: Poll 0.5s: select undelivered → publish to SSEBroker → mark delivered_at. Publish-before-mark = at-least-once; consumers dedup on event_id. No replay for late subscribers — a client refetches state over REST; a full client queue drops events (slow consumers don't stall the broker). A deliberate UI-feed contract, not an event-sourcing bus.

nodes.label: Hexagonal Dependency Rule
nodes.rationale: domain imports nothing (plain Python + Pydantic, no clock/I-O/framework, now injected); app depends only on domain and ports; infra implements the ports and owns the composition root; api is a delivery mechanism with one code→HTTP error table. Enforced by review and import discipline.

nodes.label: Composition Root (AppContainer, env read once)
nodes.rationale: The environment is read ONLY in src/infra/container.py — env vars are 'where things are', the SQLite config table is 'how things behave'. Runtime selection is data, not deployment configuration (the old AGENT_MODE env var is gone on purpose); resolution happens per run so key rotation and agent edits apply without restarts.

nodes.label: Domain Freeze (Phase-0, 2026-07-02)
nodes.rationale: No core changes without a deliberate, recorded un-freeze. Mid-integration core churn was judged the project's top risk — the freeze is the mitigation, and it is why the rest of the system could be rebuilt around the core with confidence. Un-freezes so far: #1 AgentSpec runtime bindings (2026-07-05), #2 planning backoff gate (2026-07-08), #3 pause gate + recoverable failure (2026-07-09).

nodes.label: Planning Backoff Gate (un-freeze #2)
nodes.rationale: Plan.planning_retry_not_before + planning_attempts: a transient reasoner failure in ARCHITECTURE/ENRICHING arms a durable plan-level backoff gate — the planning-phase analog of a Task's retry_not_before — honored by the claim predicate, so a rate-limited provider makes the worker back off instead of hot-looping. Motivated by a live OpenRouter rate limit (ResourceExhausted 502) that produced a ~1s worker.tick_failed storm with no operator-visible signal. Exhausted budget or permanent failure → fail_plan → FAILED.

nodes.label: Dual-Backend Truth Test
nodes.rationale: The orchestration suite runs twice via the parametrized env_factory fixture — in-memory fakes AND the real SQLite UnitOfWork. Crash-recovery, outbox-rollback, and backoff-survives-crash passing on real SQLite is the PROOF transactional atomicity is real, not simulated by an obliging fake. Corollary discipline: fake and real adapter semantics must stay identical (detached aggregates, CAS shape, lease expiry) or the truth test is lying.

nodes.label: Known Issue H1: Double-Execution Window (lease < task timeout)
nodes.rationale: Default lease 300s < default subprocess timeout 600s, and heartbeats happen only between units — a task running >300s expires its lease, another worker re-claims and re-executes it concurrently: duplicate LLM spend and two merges of the same work.
nodes.norm_label: known issue h1: double-execution window (lease < task timeout)

nodes.label: Known Issue H2: Poisoned-Plan Starvation
nodes.rationale: The claim picks oldest updated_at; a tick exception is survived but updated_at never changes and release() doesn't touch it — a plan whose drive raises before any save is re-claimed first, every poll tick, forever, starving every other plan on a single-worker deployment.

nodes.label: SSE Bridge + Invalidate-Not-Patch Data Flow
nodes.rationale: Server state lives in React Query; SSE events don't carry state — they invalidate the affected query keys so the UI always re-reads truth instead of patching caches (payloads stay minimal by backend contract). zustand holds only what the server doesn't own. When the stream drops, a StaleNotice dims the view and reconnection triggers blanket invalidateQueries() — the refetch-on-reconnect strategy that makes SSE replay unnecessary.

nodes.label: Rate-Limit Terminal Failure (2026-07-09 live run)
nodes.rationale: A real run died terminally (GoalFailedEvent → PlanFailed) on a free-tier daily 429 rate limit with no way to pause, edit, or manually retry; the operator asked for a pause/resume button, editable goals/tasks, per-task agent log history, dependency-aware blocking, and global token/call metrics — the direct motivation for domain un-freeze #3 and the telemetry work.

nodes.label: Container-in-App Boundary Violation (old backend)
nodes.rationale: The old PlannerOrchestrator (application layer) created AppContainer.from_env() directly — application code reaching back into infrastructure. Fixed by injecting an interactive_runtime_factory so the container is created only at startup; the lesson hardened into the current rule that the environment is read only in the composition root.

nodes.label: Master Roadmap (FINAL) - Clean Domain to Launch
nodes.norm_label: master roadmap (final) - clean domain to launch

nodes.label: Working Prototype Plan - Real Reasoner + Frontend Re-point
nodes.norm_label: working prototype plan - real reasoner + frontend re-point

nodes.label: Per-agent Consumer Groups for task.assigned
nodes.rationale: A shared 'workers' consumer group delivered task.assigned to a random worker which ACKed and dropped assignments; per-agent groups on the shared stream (not per-agent streams) preserve the goal orchestrator's subscription without dual-publish.

nodes.label: Redis PEL Recovery (XAUTOCLAIM + startup replay)
nodes.rationale: Crashed consumers lost events permanently; startup replay of un-acked messages plus periodic XAUTOCLAIM makes delivery survive consumer death. Startup replay is the load-bearing fix since all groups are single-consumer after the per-agent-group change.

nodes.label: Single-writer Task State
nodes.rationale: YamlTaskRepository CAS was documented single-process-only yet 4+ processes wrote through it; workers publish task.execution_* result events and only the embedded task manager writes task state, making the file-backed CAS contract sound without a storage-engine swap.

nodes.label: Embedded Coordinators as API Lifespan Threads
nodes.rationale: Task manager, goal orchestrator, and reconciler run as lifespan threads inside the FastAPI process: one settings load, one writer process, direct SSE access; workers stay separate supervised processes so hung agents can be killed without taking the API down.
nodes.norm_label: embedded coordinators as api lifespan threads

nodes.label: Redis-to-SSE Bridge + SSEBroker Fan-out
nodes.rationale: One global SSE queue meant multiple tabs stole events and put_nowait was called from threadpool threads; the SSEBroker keeps per-client asyncio queues with thread-safe publish, and an XREAD bridge on events:all fans worker/coordinator progress to every dashboard tab.

nodes.label: Session Registry (202 + session_id endpoints)
nodes.rationale: Discovery/refine were multi-minute blocking requests with timeout-as-protocol and a permanent 409 leak; a locked SessionRegistry with explicit completion sentinels turns long planner operations into 202 + session_id with SSE progress and GET-poll fallback.

nodes.label: Architecture / Phase-review Run Endpoints
nodes.rationale: run_architecture and run_phase_review existed only in the CLI; no FastAPI route called them, so approve-architecture 409'd forever over HTTP. POST /plan/architecture/run and /plan/phase-review/run close the recurring dead-end.

nodes.label: gitignore lib/ Swallowing frontend/src/lib
nodes.rationale: A blanket Python build-artifact pattern 'lib/' in the root .gitignore silently excluded the entire frontend data layer (queries/time/layout); the committed frontend could not build and the app only ran from untracked local files.

nodes.label: Provider Tool-use Error Guard (PlannerRuntimeError classification)
nodes.rationale: Raw openai.NotFoundError (e.g. OpenRouter 404 'No endpoints found that support tool use') propagated as an opaque provider string; wrapping send_turn and re-raising PlannerRuntimeError surfaces an actionable message about tool-capable models.

nodes.label: Submit-tool Name Mismatch Bug
nodes.rationale: BasePlannerRuntime string-matched the tool name against a hardcoded submit_final_roadmap, but architecture sessions exposed submit_architecture and phase-review exposed submit_review, so submissions were never acknowledged, sessions burned all turns and FAILED, and /approve-architecture returned 409. The StubPlannerRuntime also hardcoded submit_final_roadmap, masking the bug in tests.

nodes.label: Flag-based Terminal-tool Detection
nodes.rationale: Replace fragile tool-name string matching with an explicit terminal: bool flag on PlannerTool; the runtime computes terminal_names from the tools themselves, fixing the done-signal for every session mode.

nodes.label: Auto-finalize on Budget Exhaustion
nodes.rationale: Sessions should never throw away coherent proposed work: if >=1 decision and >=1 phase were proposed, treat the session as submitted and advance to the human approval gate; hard-fail only when nothing usable was produced (require_submit=False).

nodes.label: Cooperative cancel_check User Interrupt
nodes.rationale: User interruption is cooperative and checked between turns: cancel_check callable threaded API -> orchestrator -> use case -> runtime; a cancel breaks the loop and auto-finalize salvages valid work or fails cleanly with 'cancelled by user'.

nodes.label: Typed ArchitectureRoadmap Artifact
nodes.rationale: Replace the untyped session.roadmap_data dict with a frozen pydantic ArchitectureRoadmap (phases + decisions + goal_specs) validated by a RoadmapAssembler; submit_architecture returns {accepted:false, errors} on invalid input, giving the agent a concrete self-correction loop that attacks the never-converges failure at its source. No new Milestone layer - Phase is kept.

nodes.label: Auto-start Architecture on approve-brief
nodes.rationale: approve-brief's copy promised 'starts architecture drafting' but nothing auto-started the run; launching the architecture session from approve_brief (backend-owned, guarded by the session registry) removes the dependency on a separate Draft click and survives UI closes.

nodes.label: Gate Readiness Desync (Overview vs GatePanel vs LifecycleRail)
nodes.rationale: Overview and GatePanel enabled Approve purely on plan.status === 'architecture' while only LifecycleRail correctly gated on completed decisions; approving before a completed session raised 'No completed ARCHITECTURE session found' -> 409. One source of truth: approval offered only when the session completed.
nodes.norm_label: gate readiness desync (overview vs gatepanel vs lifecyclerail)

nodes.label: GET /plan/architecture/status (reload-safe readiness)
nodes.rationale: Hydrating gate state from a status endpoint (running|completed|failed|none + decisions/phases/error) makes a page refresh mid/after-run keep the correct gate and makes the E2E deterministic (poll status instead of racing SSE).

nodes.label: Playwright architecture-flow E2E Spec
nodes.rationale: Regression guard for the 'cannot move past architecture / approve dangles on 409' bug: drive discovery -> approve brief -> auto-draft -> completed -> approve -> JIT-populated goal in the real browser against a dry-run backend.

nodes.label: Dry-run SSE Delivery Gap
nodes.rationale: Dry-run is single-process with no Redis; the Redis-to-SSE bridge is skipped, so worker/coordinator domain events never reach the browser - the UI must poll/refetch via React Query, and actual task execution is out of scope for the dry-run browser E2E.

nodes.label: Nine-phase Plan Machine
nodes.rationale: DISCOVERY -> ARCHITECTURE -> ENRICHING -> AWAITING_REVIEW -> RUNNING -> REVIEW -> DONE with REPLANNING re-entering ARCHITECTURE and FAILED terminal. ENRICHING is kept separate from ARCHITECTURE purely for crash-recovery granularity (more phase boundaries = more checkpoints); gates ALWAYS pause; RUNNING completion goes to REVIEW, not DONE.

nodes.label: Driver Model / Worker Claim Predicate
nodes.rationale: Left implicit, workers claiming conversational phases produce endless claim/release churn. Explicit matrix: DISCOVERY/REPLANNING chat-driven, ARCHITECTURE/ENRICHING/RUNNING worker-driven, gates human-command-driven; claim_one_unit's predicate = phase in {ARCHITECTURE, ENRICHING, RUNNING} plus lease-free - what isn't ready is never selected.

nodes.label: Append-only Replan Loop with Iteration Counter
nodes.rationale: Each iteration's new goals are appended; prior DONE goals stay as history AND context for the next re-plan; next_action skips terminal goals so the growing list is walked unchanged; the iteration counter increments when REPLANNING commits its new goal set.
nodes.norm_label: append-only replan loop with iteration counter

nodes.label: Abandoned-iteration Rule / Tolerant Finalize
nodes.rationale: Closes a verified resurrection bug: skip PENDING goals at request_replan AND finalize-abandon remaining non-terminal goals at REPLANNING completion; a late in-flight failure must terminal-skip (never requeue) when the plan left RUNNING, else the stale goal would re-execute after the next iteration starts.

nodes.label: Shared Failure Taxonomy
nodes.rationale: One constant set (connection_error, rate_limit, token_limit, auth_error, timeout, tool_error) used by BOTH the real runner and the dummy, so dry-run tests exercise the SAME retry/terminal paths production hits; token_limit/auth_error terminal, others retryable.

nodes.label: Outbox Relay (poller to SSE + telemetry)
nodes.rationale: Previously unspecified (r2 finding): something must actually deliver outbox rows to consumers. Simplest correct prototype is a small poller in the API process - SELECT undelivered rows in id order, push to SSE + telemetry, mark delivered; at-least-once with consumer dedup on event_id.

nodes.label: Domain Freeze after Phase 0
nodes.rationale: Mid-integration core churn is the top project risk; everything touching the domain happens before the freeze, and nothing changes the core after it without a deliberate decision-logged un-freeze.

nodes.label: Integration Truth Test (real-SQLite re-run)
nodes.rationale: Re-running the crash-recovery, outbox-rollback, and backoff-gate-survives-crash tests against the REAL SQLite UnitOfWork proves transactional atomicity is real, not simulated by the in-memory fake - the single most important verification of the integration.

nodes.label: Per-plan Lease
nodes.rationale: One worker owns a plan (sequential execution); the lease granularity IS the unit of parallelism - moving it to goal or task level is the deliberate future switch recorded in the concurrency ADR; a dead worker's lease expires and any worker reclaims from persisted state.

nodes.label: Worker Tick Reports Progress, Not Claiming
nodes.rationale: Fixes a verified hot claim/release CPU spin: worker_tick returned True whenever it claimed a plan even if drive_plan immediately returned not_ready/paused, so a single backing-off plan spun the loop; the tick must return no-progress (caller sleeps) unless work actually advanced.
nodes.norm_label: worker tick reports progress, not claiming

nodes.label: Two-method Reasoner Port (converse + enrich_goal)
nodes.rationale: The four planning methods (draft/structure/enrich/replan goals) collapse to exactly two: converse(plan, history, message, mode) -> ReasonerReply{message, goals|None} and enrich_goal(plan, goal, capabilities) -> list[Task]; goals=None means still conversing, goals set means the roadmap commit.

nodes.label: ARCHITECTURE Phase as No-LLM Passthrough
nodes.rationale: The old system needed an autonomous architecture run because its discovery produced only a brief; here the discovery conversation commits the goal roadmap itself, so an LLM re-structuring pass is redundant and risks mangling the user-agreed goal set. The phase stays in the frozen enum (REPLANNING re-enters through it; free crash checkpoint) and the handler is the seam if a real structuring pass returns.

nodes.label: JIT ENRICHING (per-goal task population)
nodes.rationale: The JIT successor is task population ONLY: one task-less goal per worker step broken into a small ordered set of plain executable tasks with capability ids; idempotent (goal with tasks = no-op) and checkpointed goal-by-goal via Signal.CONTINUE. No TDD test-writer/implementer pairing in this prototype.

nodes.label: Multi-turn Chat with Commit (MessageResponse)
nodes.rationale: DISCOVERY/REPLANNING are real multi-turn chat: user messages persist BEFORE the LLM call (they survive reasoner crashes), a reply without goals keeps the conversation open, a reply with goals is the roadmap commit; the chat reply travels in the HTTP response body (MessageResponse{reply, committed, phase}) - SSE carries only domain events, no dual-publish.

nodes.label: Providers Catalog Credential Resolution
nodes.rationale: LLM credentials/model resolve via the SQLite providers catalog (provider/model rows + envelope-encrypted secrets + config keys), never env vars at runtime; llm mode fail-fasts with REASONER_CONFIG_INVALID -> 422, and stub mode never touches the secret store so dry-run needs no master key.

nodes.label: Tool-calling Agent Loop (run_tool_session)
nodes.rationale: Ported from the old planner runtime (git show 8f30306) 'identical but better': terminal submit tools end the session, {accepted:false, errors} feeds back for self-correction, plain text ends conversational sessions, budget exhaustion raises a transient ReasonerError; AsyncOpenAI with transient/permanent retry classification and the empty-choices guard.

nodes.label: StubReasoner (deterministic ask:/goal: grammar)
nodes.rationale: Drives dry-run and tests deterministically: 'ask:' lines yield question turns (goals=None), the goal:/task: grammar commits the roadmap; the stub implements the full new port so dry-run keeps driving all 9 phases without a key.

nodes.label: OpenAIReasoner
nodes.rationale: converse replays history as plain text (never provider transcripts - immune to dangling-tool-call issues and provider switches); a text reply without submit IS the question, so no ask_question tool is needed; enrich_goal uses submit_tasks with a short budget and unknown-capability self-correction. Handlers re-validate all tool args - never trust provider schema enforcement.

nodes.label: Double-execution Window (H1)
nodes.rationale: Defaults allow a 600s task under a 300s lease with no mid-run heartbeat; with two workers a >300s task exceeds its lease and is claimed and executed a second time concurrently - the standing chaos failure. Fix: mid-run asyncio heartbeat + boot-time lease/timeout validation.

nodes.label: Poisoned-plan Starvation (H2)
nodes.rationale: Claim ORDER BY updated_at + release() not touching updated_at means a plan whose drive raises pre-save is re-claimed first every second forever, starving all other plans on a single-worker deployment; fix is rotating failing plans to the back of the claim queue by setting updated_at on release.

nodes.label: Bounded Task Attempt History
nodes.rationale: Requeue erases the failed attempt's result (task.result = None), leaving the failure trail only in outbox payloads; keeping a bounded attempt_history answers 'what happened to attempt 2 of task X' from the API alone. Requires a deliberate minimal domain un-freeze, following the AgentSpec precedent; the alternative (history in agent_events only) loses transactionality with the state that produced it.

nodes.label: Three Futures (Surgeon / Architect / Heretic)
nodes.rationale: Surgeon: fix six operational defects in 1-2 weeks, forecloses nothing. Architect: task-level execution pool the system is 'secretly becoming' (unused depends_on DAG seam) - medium-high risk, forecloses the one-document-is-the-whole-truth simplicity. Heretic (Temporal/DBOS): NOT worth it - local-first SQLite deployment, the human-gated 9-phase machine gets harder to test, and the dual-backend truth suite already provides the atomicity proof an engine would.

nodes.label: Old Plan Lifecycle (discovery -> architecture -> phase_active -> phase_review -> done)
nodes.rationale: The pre-refactor progression driven by CLI/API planner sessions (plan init / architect / review), each a background run with persisted session records, decision/phase proposals, and operator y/n/edit approval; phases dispatched goals.
nodes.norm_label: old plan lifecycle (discovery -> architecture -> phase_active -> phase_review -> done)

nodes.label: Reconciler (expired leases, stuck tasks)
nodes.rationale: Detected expired leases, stuck tasks and operational drift and requeued/failed stale work; deleted in the refactor - its flow half moved to the pull-scan and its crash half to lease expiry.

nodes.label: Project Spec Governance (propose/diff/apply)
nodes.rationale: project_spec.yaml was the canonical versioned source of architecture constraints; mutation was operator-mediated only (spec propose -> diff -> apply, agents could never write it), preventing agents from silently mutating architectural constraints during execution. Shelved: governance, not core-flow critical, and the spec endpoints were a source of real bugs.

nodes.label: TaskGraphOrchestrator (goal orchestrator)
nodes.rationale: Event-driven goal orchestration: started goals on assignment or dependency release, merged completed task branches into the goal branch, triggered JIT task planning on goal.unblocked, and opened PRs at READY_FOR_REVIEW.

nodes.label: GitHub PR Gate
nodes.rationale: Opened idempotent PRs at READY_FOR_REVIEW, polled checks/approval, turned PR state into goal transitions - the orchestrator never merged PRs, humans did. Shelved because local outputs cover the prototype and GitHub coupling added a token, polling loops, and a whole failure domain; the Workspace port is the reintroduction seam (PR as an output strategy beside branch-merge).

nodes.label: Decision Gate & Decision History
nodes.rationale: Explicit architectural decisions as first-class approvable artifacts with a queryable per-project history - 'genuine whitespace'. Shelved because the discovery conversation absorbed the negotiation function; worth reintroducing as a lightweight Decision artifact on the plan document (submit_goals could grow a decisions field) rather than a new phase - an explicit reviewable 'why' ledger next to the 'what'.

nodes.label: Redis Event Topology
nodes.rationale: Redis streams carried all coordination (per-agent consumer groups, XAUTOCLAIM/PEL recovery, Redis-to-SSE bridge, single-writer discipline). Deleted: the SQLite lease + transactional outbox provide the same guarantees with one moving part and real transactions; Redis remains a roadmap item only if multi-machine workers become real, with the repository port as the swap point.

nodes.label: Planner Sessions (202/session/poll machinery)
nodes.rationale: The session machinery was the system's chronic failure locus (the 409 dead-end, the terminal-tool name mismatch, timeout-as-protocol, the gate desync trilogy) - sessions were state about planning living outside the plan. The new machine makes planning state BE plan state; do NOT reintroduce sessions: a future autonomous planning pass should be a worker-driven phase like ENRICHING, not a parallel session subsystem.

nodes.label: SSE Invalidate-don't-patch Pattern
nodes.rationale: React Query owns server state, one SSE bridge keeps it fresh, zustand holds only UI state; SSE events invalidate query keys rather than patching caches because event payloads are minimal by backend contract, and reconnection triggers a blanket refetch - which is why the backend doesn't need SSE replay.
